title: Materials Engineering
shortSummary: The materials that survive spaceflight — superalloys, composites, thermal protection systems, and why material selection is a life-or-death decision.
whyItMatters: Rockets operate at the extremes of temperature, pressure, and vibration. The wrong material choice can mean catastrophic failure. From SpaceX's use of carbon fibre composites to NASA's Space Shuttle thermal tiles, materials engineering defines what is physically possible.
